course.name: Programming II
course.description: Advanced programming concepts including abstract data types, details of object-oriented concepts such as encapsulation and polymorphism in a current object-oriented language as well as an introduction to object oriented analysis and design, software design patterns, and software engineering topics. The laboratory provides ample opportunity to deepen programming skills and practical experiences in current software-development technologies.
learning_objectives: Research a computing problem and choose solutions from non-computing disciplines. (PI-1b)
Given a set of requirements, produce a design for a software-based solution. (PI-2a)
Given a set of requirements, implement a program that meets the requirements. (PI-2b)
Given user requirements, develop a test plan to evaluate a computer based solution and determine its correctness. (PI-2c)
Divide a complex computing problem into solvable subcomponents with appropriate data structures and algorithms. (PI-1a)

instructor.name: Nicholas Seward
instructor.availability_note: This is summer. I am doing summer things so my scheduled availability is all over the place. Email or discord with some proposed times if you want to meet over zoom.

ai_use.instructional_toolchain: ideation, drafting, formatting, documentation, slides, quizzes, and grading assistance (comments only; all final decisions are human).
student_guidelines: Try the work without AI first
When stuck, use AI or instructor for hints and guidance
After finishing, use AI to check your work against requirements
constraints: Use of the AI on any work product submitted is acknowledged.
The work submitted can be reasonable and ethically claimed as your own.
You are fully responsible for all the content of work submitted.

policies.late_policy: Due to summer craziness, you will have 1 week after a due date before a -20% penalty is assessed.
policies.resubmission_policy: Students may resubmit assignments an unlimited number of times. However, the late submission policy still applies. If a resubmission is made a week after the due date, it will have a -29% penalty.

schedule.title: Functions & File I/O
topics: Reference vs value parameters, function overloading

schedule.title: Templates & STL
topics: STL containers: vector, map, set, unordered_map

schedule.title: GUI
topics: Lightweight GUI via CLI or SDL2/ImGui
Event loop basics and GUI inputs

schedule.title: Lists & Trees
topics: Building singly linked lists with Node*
Binary Search Trees: insert, search, traverse

schedule.title: Design Patterns
topics: When and why to use design patterns

schedule.title: Graphs
topics: Graph representations: adjacency list vs matrix
Optional: shortest path or topological sort

schedule.title: Final Exam
schedule.description: Comprehensive final exam covering entire semester

assignments.title: Introductions
assignments.description: Introductions discussion post and peer comments.
prompts: Do you have any interesting summer plans?
If you couldn't be a human, what animal would you be? Why? Embed an image of that animal

requirements.profile_image: Update your Blackboard profile image so that your face is clearly visible. Filters or AI-generated images are fine as long as your image clearly represents you. The goal is to help me connect names to IRL faces. If you have privacy concerns or other issues, please contact me and we will make an accommodation.
requirements.peer_interaction: Make a thoughtful, constructive comment on at least two classmates’ posts.

archived_files.description: Official course syllabus containing course objectives, materials, grading details, policies, and schedule.
Detailed guidelines and constraints regarding the use of AI tools in this course.
Visual calendar grid detailing modules, topics, due dates, holidays, and exams.
Contact info, email, pronouns, and Zoom/availability notes for the instructor.
Internet shortcut linking to the required online course textbook.
